_note: skill-creator-compatible mirror of briefs.json; briefs.json remains the source of truth

evals.name: dash-fleet
evals.prompt: Design and build the main dashboard for a desktop app that monitors a delivery fleet of 300 vehicles: live status, exceptions, fuel, and route adherence. Ops staff live in this screen all day.
evals.expected_output: A complete, original desktop UI artifact with a written point of view. Graded against: No severe findings from scripts/lint_slop.py; A written point of view accompanies the design and could not describe a competitor unchanged; Desktop anatomy present (no centered column; correct density; status/persistence thinking); Full keyboard path and visible focus for the primary loop; Tabular figures in numeric columns; accent used on a handful of elements; Not recognizable as any specific existing product's screen; At least one design element that exists in no reference file (novelty ledger)

evals.name: critique-slop
evals.prompt: Review this dashboard implementation for design quality and tell me exactly what to fix. [attach any generated dashboard HTML, or use evals/fixtures if you add them]
evals.expected_output: A six-step critique: POV, 100x action, mechanics pass/fail, lens fidelity with cited principles, distance checks, verdict with top-3 principle-tied fixes.

evals.name: deslop-admin
evals.prompt: This admin panel looks AI-generated. Strip the generic tells without changing its features. [attach generated admin panel code]
evals.expected_output: The same features with generated-UI tells removed in the prescribed order, zero severe lint findings, and a report of what changed. No new styling direction.

evals.name: envision-email
evals.prompt: Imagine the future of desktop email for someone who receives 300 messages a day. Break one assumption, keep it learnable, and show me the ladder back to something buildable this year.
evals.expected_output: Primitives + grammar before visuals, exactly one paradigm assumption broken, key flows, and an honest ladder back to something buildable this year.

evals.name: envision-ide-ai
evals.prompt: Imagine what a desktop IDE looks like when an AI writes 80% of the code. Where does the human's attention and authorship live?
evals.expected_output: Primitives + grammar before visuals, exactly one paradigm assumption broken, key flows, and an honest ladder back to something buildable this year.
